text: ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced that in response to U.S.‑Israeli attacks on the Karaj B1 bridge and Mahshahr petrochemical plants, it has carried out the first phase of retaliatory strikes under Wave 96 of Operation Truthful Promise 4. Targets included a refinery supplying fuel to Israeli fighter jets in Haifa, Exxon/Mobil/Chevron gas facilities in Habshan (UAE), a US petrochemical in Al Ruwais (UAE), a U.S. petrochemical in Sitra (Bahrain), and a U.S. petrochemical in Shu'aiba (Kuwait). The IRGC warned that if attacks on non‑military targets continue, the second phase will be much more crushing and widespread. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Iran’s government spokesperson Fatemeh Mohajerani said that since the start of the war, 105,125 civilian units have been damaged, including 83,351 residential units and 39,508 commercial‑residential units in Tehran. A total of 322 health centres, 763 schools and 55 libraries across 12 provinces have also been hit. The spokesperson condemned the attack on the Delaram Sina psychiatric hospital as a war crime, and said the strike on the Mahshahr petrochemical plant killed five people and wounded 170. She urged Iranians not to fall for “false claims” by the U.S. and Israel. @wfwitness

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Iran’s Government spokesperson: According to the Red Crescent, so far 105,1325 civilian units including residential homes, commercial units, educational and cultural centers have been damaged; also 322 medical centers, 763 schools, and 55 libraries in 12 provinces have been damaged.

↩️ Reply to OSINT Michael A. Horowitz RT @modgovae : UAE Air Defenses engaged 9 Ballistic Missiles and 1 Cruise missile and 50 UAV’s. The Ministry of Defense announced that on April 5, 2026, UAE air defense systems successfully engaged 9 Ballistic missiles and 1 Cruise missile and 50 UAV’s launched from Iran. Since the onset of these blatant Iranian attacks, UAE air defenses have engaged a total of 507 ballistic missiles, 24 cruise missiles, and 2,191 UAV’s. No martyrs, fatalities, or injuries have been reported in the past hours. Since the onset of the blatant Iranian attacks, the total number of martyrs has reached 2, in addition to the martyrdom of a Moroccan civilian contracted with the Armed Forces. The total number of civilian fatalities stands at 10, from Pakistani, Nepalese, Bangladeshi, Palestinian, Indian, and Egyptian nationalities. The total number of injuries has reached 217 since the onset of the blatant Iranian attacks, involving individuals of various nationalities, including Emirati, Egyptian, Sudanese, Ethiopian, Filipino, Pakistani, Iranian, Indian, Bangladeshi, Sri Lankan, Azerbaijani, Yemeni, Ugandan, Eritrean, Lebanese, Afghan, Bahraini, Comorian, Turkish, Iraqi, Nepalese, Nigerian, Omani, Jordanian, Palestinian, Ghanaian, Indonesian, Swedish, Tunisian, Moroccan, and Russian. The Ministry of Defence affirmed that it remains fully prepared and ready to deal with any threats and will firmly confront anything that aims to undermine the security of the country, in a manner that ensures the protection of its sovereignty, security and stability and safeguards its interests and national capabilities. #وزارة_الدفاع #وزارة_الدفاع_الإماراتية #MOD #UAEMinistryOfDefence tweet

Open Source Intel THE BIG STORY 🔴 CIA ran a deception effort inside Iran, pushing a false narrative that the downed airman had already been found and was being moved on the ground. While Iranian forces chased that story, the CIA identified his real location in the mountains and passed it to the White House, where President Trump ordered the rescue. F15E SHOOTDOWN AND CSAR OPERATION — FULL VERIFIED BREAKDOWN APRIL 3 — THE SHOOTDOWN F15E from the 494th Fighter Squadron out of RAF Lakenheath is hit over southwestern Iran. Crash site confirmed in Kohgiluyeh and Boyer Ahmad Province near Dehdasht. Wreckage includes ACES II ejection seat and tail section matching the squadron. Cause Iran claims it used a new IRGC air defense system. Exact weapon system remains unconfirmed. HOUR 0 Both crew eject. Pilot activates survival radio and PRD immediately. Contact established with U.S. aircraft overhead. Pilot recovered within hours. WSO lands separately in mountainous terrain. WSO beacon briefly detected by satellites, then goes silent. Reason unknown. HOURS 1 TO 12 WSO begins evasion using SERE training. Maintains intermittent encrypted communication with U.S. forces. IRGC launches large scale search operation. Iranian state TV urges civilians to report or capture the pilot. Rewards up to $60,000 reported. Public messaging includes calls to shoot on sight. HOURS 12 TO 24 WSO moves into high elevation terrain. Climbs to around 7,000 feet. U.S. ISR tracks Iranian movement continuously. MQ9 Reaper drones strike Iranian personnel approaching within roughly 3 km. U.S. aircraft strike IRGC forces moving toward the area. HOURS 24 TO 36 A10 INCIDENT A10 supporting the mission is hit. Pilot exits Iranian airspace, ejects over Kuwait, recovered safely. CSAR AIR OPERATIONS HC130 conducts aerial refueling missions. Black Hawk helicopters take fire during rescue operations. Aircraft return safely with some injuries onboard. CIA DECEPTION OPERATION CIA launches deception campaign inside Iran. False reports spread that the WSO had already been recovered. CIA locates the WSO and passes coordinates to U.S. leadership. President Trump authorizes immediate rescue. HOURS 36 TO 48 — FINAL EXTRACTION Large scale nighttime rescue launched. U.S. special operations forces inserted. WSO located and recovered alive after sustained evasion. EXFIL COMPLICATIONS MC130 aircraft land at remote airstrip inside Iran. At least 2 aircraft become disabled on the ground. Decision made to destroy them. Aircraft blown to prevent capture of sensitive equipment. U.S. assessment Aircraft were self destroyed, not shot down. Reports indicate a light helicopter was also lost at the site. Replacement aircraft deployed. All personnel extracted successfully. Israel pauses airstrikes during the rescue. TRUMP STATEMENT President Trump confirms success. No U.S. personnel killed. Dozens of aircraft involved. FINAL OUTCOME 2 crew from the F15E rescued 1 A10 pilot rescued 3 U.S. airmen All alive No captures Mission completed deep inside Iran under hostile conditions. - Open Source Intel tweet

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said the petrochemical industry has generated approximately $18 billion for the IRGC over the past two years, directly serving Iran’s weapons production. “As long as missile fire continues against Israeli citizens, Iran will pay painful prices that will erode and collapse its national infrastructure,” he stated. Katz also thanked American partners for safely rescuing their air crew, calling it another expression of close US‑Israel cooperation. The minister confirmed that the Israeli Air Force struck central petrochemical sites yesterday, following strikes on steel plants and other national infrastructure. He added that Israel will continue to target Iran’s leadership, security objectives and strategic assets across the country. @wfwitness

Visioner 🇺🇸 🚁 ✈️ Details of the brilliantly executed operation by the USA last night: Due to the inability to extract the damaged MC-130J Commando II aircraft, American special forces destroyed the flying machines on a runway-like path at the site. Later, geolocation of video footage showing the debris, filmed and shared by locals, reveals that the evacuation team had penetrated 295 kilometers deep from Iran's western border. 🪖 This indescribable operation was carried out to rescue and evacuate a single American pilot who had been hiding in the mountainous terrain of Iran from Friday morning until dawn today. See the latest updates with us: @visionergeo tweet

Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : 🇺🇸 🚁 ✈️ Details of last night’s U.S. operation: unable to extract the damaged MC-130J Commando II aircraft, special forces destroyed them on a makeshift runway at the site. 🛰️ Geolocation of locally filmed wreckage indicates the evacuation team operated approximately 295 km inside Iran from the western border. 🪖 The operation was carried out to rescue a single U.S. pilot who had been hiding in mountainous terrain from Friday morning until early today. See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

↩️ Reply to OSINT Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : ❗ ❗ 🇷🇺 🇮🇷 Russian and Iranian propaganda outlets are claiming the U.S. concealed additional losses, including possible casualties aboard helicopters not initially reported. 🟥 However, MH-6 Little Bird helicopters are commonly transported aboard MC-130J Commando II aircraft in U.S. special operations, and several technical factors explain their destruction on site: ▪️ MH-6 helicopters are designed to be compact; for transport inside a C-130, their main and tail rotors are removed, along with external mounts if needed. ▪️ A single MC-130 can carry two such helicopters, which can be reassembled and made operational within 15–20 minutes by trained crews. ▪️ If extraction fails, equipment is destroyed due to: 1.Time constraints — reloading and securing helicopters takes too long during active combat conditions 2.Aircraft incapacity — if the MC-130 is damaged, stuck, or unable to take off 3.“Zero-Zero” protocol — forces destroy all equipment to prevent enemy use or propaganda exploitation ▪️ Helicopters are typically destroyed using thermite charges, which melt engines and critical electronics. See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness ⚡ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Based on the wreckage we can say that at least a HC-130J and an unknown helicopter were blown up by US SOF in Iran. Four C-130 engines can be seen all in line suggesting they belonged to the same aircraft. The rotor of an helicopter can be seen nearby. Its unclear how many blades it has. Black Hawks have four blades. This one seems to have six although this could be due to the poor shape of the wreck. In what was likely the most complex search and rescue operation, the US Air Force lost at least three aircrafts, likely four, to save the life of two stranded crew. This is the second time US forces have blown up their C-130 and helicopter in the middle of Iran during a rescue attempt. @wfwitness
